summary_one_line: Input must declare/use an XML ID attribute (e.g. id="...") so cur->ids becomes non-NULL when the doc is freed.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input bytes that spell an ID-typed attribute literal such as the substring "id=\"" (and/or an ID-declaring DTD), causing libxml2 to register an ID table on the document so cur->ids != NULL at free time and the true branch at line 472 is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal "xml:id=\"" / ":id=\"" attribute-name bytes at offsets 0x0e-0x1d (Seed 1: 6d 6c 3a 69 64 3d 22 = 'ml:id="'), and the winner takes the true side 4/0 at line 472 while the loser is 0/10; only the winner registers an ID table.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds spend their bytes on unrelated structure (ELEMENT/PCDATA/xlink/IMP fragments, repeated filler runs) and never spell an ID attribute literal, so cur->ids stays NULL and line 472 stays at T=0 F=10 across all 10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator over plain value_profile; cmplog buffers the runtime CMP operands from libxml2's attribute-name/type comparisons and splices the matching bytes back into the input at the offset that fed the comparison, manufacturing the exact "id" attribute literal that registers the ID table. Plain value_profile has only the CMP_MAP distance gradient and havoc, so it cannot place the literal and stays blocked.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side requires cur->ids to be non-NULL, i.e. the document must have an ID table.
value_profile_cmplog wins this branch over value_profile with full separation; the delta technique is I2S.
The winner resolved all 10 trials while the loser was blocked all 10 trials.
A winner-resolving seed contains the ID-attribute literal bytes spelling 'ml:id="'.
On the call chain the blocker line 472 takes true only for the winner (W T=4) and never for the loser (L T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L 472  T=4 F=0  T=0 F=10  if (cur->ids != NULL) xmlFreeIDTable((xmlIDTablePtr) cur-...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of the havoc/token stack.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 7249 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 31a0f4cf800d3991`; the 13-level ancestor chain contains one I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 12, the ParentInfo-only signature of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where value_profile resolves the cur->ids!=NULL branch at the same rate as value_profile_cmplog despite requiring an exact 'id'-attribute literal to register the ID table, showing the CMP_MAP gradient alone places the literal without I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from the observed 'id="' literal bytes to ID-table registration is inferred from libxml2 semantics and source flow, not directly traced from the seed byte to the xmlAddID call site in the prompt.
